title: Lisbon 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Lisbon 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Lisbon, Portugal: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Belém Tower and Jerónimos Monastery. Prices from $1821 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Lisbon, Portugal — Mediterranean climate with mild, rainy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C), moderated by Atlantic breezes. March to May and September to October offer warm, sunny days without the peak-summer heat and crowds. This itinerary blends Belém Tower, Jerónimos Monastery, Alfama district with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Belém Tower, Jerónimos Monastery, Alfama district
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Lisbon
itinerary.description: Land at Humberto Delgado Airport (LIS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Belém Tower — 16th-century fortified tower on the Tagus estuary. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Jerónimos Monastery & São Jorge Castle
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Jerónimos Monastery — UNESCO Manueline-style monastery. In the afternoon, continue to São Jorge Castle — Hilltop Moorish castle with panoramic views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Tram 28 ride & National Azulejo (Tile) Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tram 28 ride — Historic yellow tram through hilly old neighborhoods. In the afternoon, continue to National Azulejo (Tile) Museum — Museum dedicated to Portugal's iconic ceramic tiles. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Sintra
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Sintra, a popular side trip from Lisbon. Return to Lisbon in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Jardim Botânico Tropical — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Oceanário de Lisboa & LX Factory (paid workshops/shops)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Oceanário de Lisboa — One of Europe's largest aquariums. In the afternoon, continue to LX Factory (paid workshops/shops) — Creative hub in a converted industrial complex. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cascais
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cascais, a popular side trip from Lisbon. Return to Lisbon in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Santa Justa Lift & Carmo Convent ruins
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Santa Justa Lift — Wrought-iron elevator connecting downtown to Bairro Alto. In the afternoon, continue to Carmo Convent ruins — Earthquake-ruined Gothic convent, now an open-air museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Miradouro da Graça — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Óbidos
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Óbidos, a popular side trip from Lisbon. Return to Lisbon in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Sintra's Pena Palace (day trip) & Alfama district
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sintra's Pena Palace (day trip) — Colorful romanticist palace on a hilltop. In the afternoon, continue to Alfama district — Oldest neighborhood with narrow lanes and fado music. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Lisbon: Miradouro da Senhora do Monte & Praça do Comércio
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Miradouro da Senhora do Monte — Best free panoramic viewpoint over the city. In the afternoon, continue to Praça do Comércio — Grand riverside square. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Fado houses in Alfama backstreets — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Time Out Market (browsing) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to LIS for your departure flight.

travelTips: Ride Tram 28 early morning to avoid the worst crowds and pickpocket risk.
Book Sintra's Pena Palace tickets online with a timed slot — queues can take hours in peak season.
The Lisboa Card bundles transit and discounted museum entry, worth it for 2+ day visits.
Try ginjinha (cherry liqueur) from a hole-in-the-wall bar in Rossio for an authentic cheap experience.
Sintra day trips are best started early (before 9:30am) to beat tour bus crowds.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable shoes with grip for steep, slippery cobblestone hills
Light layers for cool mornings/evenings even in summer
A light rain jacket for winter visits
thingsToAvoid: Pickpockets on the crowded Tram 28, a well-known target route.
Renting a car for Sintra — parking is scarce; take the train from Rossio instead.
Restaurants directly on tourist strips near Praça do Comércio — better food is a few streets inland.
Walking Alfama's steep alleys in flip-flops or smooth-soled shoes — cobblestones get slick.
Assuming credit cards work everywhere — small taverns and kiosks often prefer cash.
topRestaurants: Time Out Market — Food Hall / Various ($$)
Taberna da Rua das Flores — Portuguese Petiscos ($$)
Restaurante O Prego da Peixaria — Portuguese ($$)
instagramSpots: Miradouro da Senhora do Monte at sunset
adventureActivities: Cycling along the Tagus riverside path
luxuryExperiences: Private Sintra palace tour with driver
Fado dinner show at an exclusive Alfama venue
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Miradouro de Santa Luzia
Private fado music dinner in Alfama
Romantic day trip to Sintra's pastel palaces
Tagus river sunset cruise for two
budgetExperiences: Ride historic Tram 28 for a few euros
Pastéis de Belém custard tarts for a cheap treat
Wander Alfama's free open-air fado streets

festivalCalendar.name: New Year's Eve fireworks at Praça do Comércio

localEtiquette: A relaxed pace of life is the norm — meals and service are unhurried.
Greet with "Bom dia/Boa tarde" and a light kiss on both cheeks among friends (not strangers).
Tipping around 5-10% is appreciated but not obligatory.
Avoid comparing Portugal to Spain — locals take pride in a distinct identity and language.
Dress modestly when visiting churches and monasteries.
localTransportGuide: Lisbon's hilly terrain is eased by iconic trams, funiculars and the Santa Justa Lift; the metro covers longer distances efficiently. A Viva Viagem card works across trams, buses, metro and the funiculars, and Uber/Bolt are widely used for late-night or hill-heavy trips.
bestTimeToVisit: March to May and September to October offer warm, sunny days without the peak-summer heat and crowds.
weather: Mediterranean climate with mild, rainy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C), moderated by Atlantic breezes.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~11.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Lisbon?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Lisbon?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to October offer warm, sunny days without the peak-summer heat and crowds. Mediterranean climate with mild, rainy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C), moderated by Atlantic breezes.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Lisbon safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Lisbon is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Lisbon?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Nadia Johnson
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Lisbon is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Carmo Convent ruins. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Elena Santos
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond LX Factory grounds and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Tom Novak
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Alfama district and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Liam Sharma
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Lisbon was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Oceanário de Lisboa was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mia Brown
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Oceanário de Lisboa and Tram 28 ride. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ethan Smith
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Sintra's Pena Palace (day trip) and Alfama district. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Grace Khan
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful

reviews.author: Wei Costa
